name: Nidwalden (Switzerland)
description: A square red field charged with an upright white key with a doubled bow.
history: A single key appears in the Nidwalden seal from the mid-13th century; the double-warded key was used on its war flag from the early 15th century. The key is one of the Keys of Heaven of Unterwalden.

features.role: upright key with a doubled bow
features.symbolism: the Keys of Heaven (Saint Peter) of Unterwalden
